data.title: Richmond American Homes introduces two new communities in Victorville: Astra & Solstice at West Ridge.
data.description: A wide range of new homes is on display at Astra & Solstice in West Ridge, located in Victorville, California. This showcase features an impressive selection of properties brought to you by Richmond American Homes of Maryland, Inc. This company operates as a licensed Real Estate Broker under the California Department of Real Estate, with Corporation License Number 01842595. A subsidiary of Sekisui, these homes offer a unique opportunity for homebuyers in the area.

data.title: BridgeBio Pharma approved equity grants to 34 new employees in restricted stock units totaling 76,701 shares on Feb 12, 2026. Vests quarterly until Feb 16, 2027.
data.description: BridgeBio Pharma, Inc., a biopharmaceutical company from Palo Alto, California, made an announcement on February 17, 2026, regarding equity grants to 34 new employees. The compensation committee of BridgeBio approved these grants in the form of restricted stock units, totaling 76,701 shares of the company's common stock. Vesting of these shares will occur over time, with one-fourth vested on February 16, 2027, and the remaining shares vesting quarterly thereafter. The vesting is subject to the employees' continued employment with BridgeBio or its subsidiaries on the specified dates. The company is listed on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol BBIO.

MICHAEL BARR SAYS FEDERAL RESERVE SHOULD KEEP INTEREST RATES STEADY FOR SOME TIME UNTIL CLEARER EVIDENCE SHOWS INFLATION IS DURABLY HEADING BACK TO THE 2% TARGET WHILE OFFICIALS MONITOR RISKS TO THE LABOR MARKET AND ECONOMY.

data.title: Voloridge acquires property at Harbourside Place in Jupiter, FL, expanding its local presence. Feb. 17, 2026 - PRNewswire.
data.description: In Jupiter, Florida, on February 17, 2026, Voloridge, a prominent player in quantitative investment management and data science advancements in health and wellness, has completed the acquisition of a section of Harbourside Place in the area. This move represents a notable increase in Voloridge's footprint locally, reinforcing its commitment to the region.

data.title: SEI Network sees growth with Toku payroll integration, Monaco CLOB launch, and 26% rise in daily active addresses to 1M users in Q4.
data.description: The SEI ecosystem has grown with the integration of Toku Payroll and the launch of Monaco CLOB, in addition to the release of Q4 data revealing a 26% increase in daily active addresses, bringing the total number of users to 1 million.

data.title: North Island Credit Union offering relief programs to members affected by federal shutdown in San Diego; announced on Feb. 17, 2026.
data.description: North Island Credit Union has recently disclosed their decision to provide special assistance to their members who are federal employees residing in the North Island area. The credit union aims to offer relief programs to aid those experiencing pay disruptions. This announcement was made on February 17, 2026, via PR Newswire. This initiative is directed towards supporting the affected members during these challenging times.

data.title: Argo Corp raised $5.4M in funding, with co-founders contributing $1.7M, agreeing not to sell 29.47M shares until 2028.
data.description: Toronto, February 17, 2026 - Argo Corporation, a leading company in innovative transit solutions, announces that it closed $5,445,383 in financing, which included $1,768,200 from its co-founders Praveen Arichandran and Qamar Qureshi. Both co-founders have agreed to voluntary lock-up agreements with the company, restricting them from selling or disposing of any of the 29.47 million shares resulting from common share purchase warrants until February 8, 2028, with limited exceptions. This move is to ensure the stability of the company's shares and investments. Argo is listed on TSXV as ARGH and OTCQX as ARGHF.

data.title: Armada Acquisition Corp. III prices $225 million IPO, offering 22.5 million units at $10 each on Nasdaq, with ticker symbol "AACIU."
data.description: Armada Acquisition Corp. III, referred to as AACI, has disclosed that it has priced its initial public offering at $10.00 per unit, with a total of 22,500,000 units being offered. The units are scheduled to be listed on the Nasdaq Global Market under the ticker symbol "AACIU" starting from February 18, 2026. Each unit comprises one Class A ordinary share along with half of one redeemable warrant. Holders of the whole warrant are entitled to purchase one Class A ordinary share.

data.title: Flashpoint Therapeutics announces significant publication on new HPV cancer vaccine using Spherical Nucleic Acid platform. Led by Professor Chad A. Mirkin of Northwestern University.
data.description: Flashpoint Therapeutics, a biotech company based in Evanston, Illinois, has released findings from a significant peer-reviewed publication showcasing the effectiveness of its Spherical Nucleic Acid (SNA) platform in the field of immuno-oncology. The research, spearheaded by Flashpoint’s scientific co-founder Professor Chad A. Mirkin at the International Institute for Nanotechnology at Northwestern University, highlights a groundbreaking therapeutic vaccine. This vaccine has shown the ability to produce a strong and targeted immune response. The study marks a notable advancement in nanomedicine and its potential application in cancer treatment.

data.title: Burlington Stores, Inc. expands to 206,392 sq ft at 1400 Broadway; Nespresso renews lease for 41,835 sq ft at 111 W. 33rd Street.
data.description: Empire State Realty Trust, Inc. revealed today that it secured deals with Burlington Stores, Inc. and Nespresso for lease renewals and expansions at prime Manhattan office locations in Q4 2025. Burlington Stores, Inc. committed to an expansion lease for a full floor spanning 35,629 square feet, alongside an early renewal for its existing 170,763 square foot space, bringing its total footprint to 206,392 square feet at 1400 Broadway.

data.title: Dream Industrial REIT had a successful Q4 2025, reporting 5% FFO per Unit growth. They will discuss results on a call on Feb 18, 2026.
data.description: In Toronto, Dream Industrial Real Estate Investment Trust (DIR.UN-TSX) revealed its financial outcomes for the quarter and year concluding on December 31, 2025. The REIT reported a 5% growth in FFO per Unit. A conference call to delve into these results is scheduled by management for February 18, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. Eastern Time. The Trust expressed satisfaction with its performance in 2025, highlighting a 70...

data.title: Ether bulls aim for $2.5K with staking ETF debut and rising RWA market cap, indicating growing adoption and investor interest in Ethereum.
data.description: Increasing numbers of institutional investors are showing interest in Ether, with major endowments moving significant amounts of capital towards the cryptocurrency. BlackRock, a prominent investment firm, has even launched an ETF focused on staking within the Ether ecosystem. This move signifies a growing trend of real-world assets being tokenized on the Ethereum blockchain, attracting traditional finance investors to the space.

data.title: b1BANK teams up with Covecta for AI integration in banking, aiming to boost efficiency and compliance.
data.description: Through a long-term collaboration, b1BANK will incorporate advanced AI technology into various stages of its banking processes. This partnership aims to enhance efficiency, strengthen oversight, and boost output for teams within the company. The integration of agentic AI is projected to facilitate faster operations, improved controls, and increased capacity. Furthermore, this initiative will uphold robust risk management strategies and compliance standards within the organization.

data.title: Investigation into Brown & Brown Inc. (NYSE: BRO) for potential securities law violations, urging investors with substantial losses to contact Edelson Lechtzin LLP.
data.description: A law firm based in Newtown, Pennsylvania, known as Edelson Lechtzin LLP, has initiated an inquiry into potential breaches of federal securities laws related to Brown & Brown, Inc. ("Brown & Brown") (NYSE: BRO). This investigation stems from accusations suggesting that Brown & Brown may have furnished misleading information about their business operations. The examination is centered on scrutinizing the activities of Brown & Brown in light of these allegations.

data.title: Canfor Pulp Products Inc. to record $106 million impairment charge in Q4 2025 due to declining pulp prices and fiber issues.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on February 17, 2026, Canfor Pulp Products Inc. ("CPPI") revealed plans to report a non-cash asset write-down and impairment charge of around $106 million in its fourth quarter results for 2025. This impairment is a result of continuing drops in global US-dollar pulp list prices and ongoing difficulties in obtaining economically feasible fiber sources.

data.title: Crown Capital Partners reported Q4 2025 net income of $1.7M, with adjusted EBITDA of $7.8M. Total revenue for the year was $98.4M.
data.description: Crown Capital Partners Inc. reported financial results for the three and twelve months ending December 31, 2025. In Q4 2025, Crown saw net income of $1.7 million compared to a net loss of $(10.9) million the previous year. Adjusted EBITDA was $7.8 million in Q4 2025, up from $0.4 million in Q4 2024. Distribution services revenue was $12.3 million, network services revenue was $21.3 million, and real estate segment revenue was $1.3 million in Q4 2025. Total assets stood at $154.5 million, and total equity at $5.2 million. The Corporation acquired PenEquity Realty Corporation on October 1, 2025. Despite missing scheduled interest payments on debentures, no immediate repayment requests have been made. The Corporation is working on disposing of assets to address liquidity needs. Crown also changed its financial year-end from December 31 to March 31. The full article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: H&P releases 2025 Sustainability Report, highlighting progress in sustainability program and performance.
data.description: TULSA, Oklahoma – Helmerich & Payne, Inc. (NYSE: HP) released its fiscal 2025 Sustainability Report, outlining the company’s sustainability initiatives, achievements, and advancements for the period concluding on September 30, 2025. The report highlights the progress made in enhancing sustainability practices across the business.
